title: Rivalry week: the grudge match nobody is calm about
arc: sustained high-energy banter wall to wall — heated, amused, interruptions throughout

lines.text: It is rivalry week, people, and I have been awake since five in the morning for no medical reason whatsoever.
He texted me a lineup diagram at five fifteen. A diagram, with arrows. This is what rivalry week does to otherwise functional adults.
Their point guard said our building is quiet. QUIET. The man shot four for nineteen here in March and he wants to talk about acoustics.
To be fair, it was quiet, because he kept missing and his own fans left early.
THANK you! That is exactly the energy this week deserves. Print the shooting splits on a billboard.
I cannot believe I am the one saying this, but the billboard budget conversation is why we cannot have nice things.
The whole game lives in one matchup: their wing against our second big on the switch. Whoever wins that possession war wins the night.
He is going to get cooked, Doc. Cooked! We have all seen the man try to slide his feet. It is like watching furniture defend.
Furniture with a seven-foot wingspan! The contest numbers say he bothers that jumper more than anyone else on the roster.
Bothering a jumper is not the same as stopping one, and you know it. This is rivalry week, we do not grade on a curve.
Fine. If he gets cooked I will read your five a.m. diagram on air, arrows and all.
Do not forget last season. The shove at the scorer's table, the waved-off buzzer beater, the coach getting tossed in the third. This fixture has receipts.
Three technicals in the last two meetings, and somehow both fan bases think the refs are against them. That is how you know a rivalry is healthy.
Healthy! Doc, someone threw a hot dog. A fully loaded hot dog. There is footage.
And the man still hit both free throws with mustard on his shoulder. Say what you want, that is professionalism.
Give me the home team by eight, a technical before halftime, and at least one moment where we both scream at the monitor.
I will take the visitors in a rock fight, and I want it on record that I predicted the hot dog sequel.
